summary: DHH expresses pleasant surprise that the pvPlayer company discovered his earlier blog criticism and responded in his comments. He takes the opportunity to directly address the pvPlayer team with specific UI improvement suggestions for their application on the Nokia 3650. The post demonstrates early blogging culture where companies would discover and engage with customer feedback through weblogs.
key_points: The pvPlayer company found DHH's earlier criticism through blog comments, which impressed him
Company responsiveness to weblog feedback increased his willingness to recommend the product
He uses the attention to propose specific UI changes that could elevate the app from 'horrible' to 'great'
money_quotes: What a pleasent surprise to have my opinions discovered by the company itself.
It makes me even more comfortable recommending pvPlayer when I know the company listens its customers and checks weblogs.
key_insight: Companies that actively monitor and respond to customer feedback on blogs earn stronger advocacy from their users.
